Description: 6-week program; gender-specific sessions
Notes: 6-week program; gender-specific sessions

Description: Licensed residential social model program for adult men. Offers individual and group counselling, peer support, 12-step meeting attendance, case management, and social/recreational activities. Open-ended length of stay.
Notes: Non-smoking

Organization: Fraser Health / Maple Ridge Treatment Centre (IRT)
Description: Fraser Health residential treatment program offering 4-week and 7-week intensive programs for problematic substance use. Evidence-based, holistic, and group-based with some individual and family sessions.
Notes: Higher-confidence entry from treatment-centre PDF.

Description: Comprehensive 90-day treatment program focused on recovery, daily structure, and aftercare planning. Extended care may be available.
Notes: Non-smoking; replacement therapy permitted.

Description: Indigenous-focused recovery program ranging from 90 days to 1 year. Includes whole-person programming with life skills, employment readiness, drum circles, sweats, and healing walks.
Notes: Second stage program

Description: Younger adult stabilization and transitional living home for men with current or recent mild to moderate substance use issues. Offers recovery groups, exercise groups, life skills, and goal setting.
Notes: Higher-confidence entry from treatment-centre PDF. Transitional/supportive model rather than classic intensive residential treatment.

Description: Licensed men's recovery program with individual and group counselling, peer support, 12-step meetings, case management, and employment/education goal planning.
70-day intensive residential treatment program using a harm reduction model. Includes orientation, program development, group therapy, and transitional/aftercare planning.
60-day licensed program with individual and group counselling, peer support, 12-step meetings, case management, and employment/education planning.

10-week residential treatment program for women wanting to stop the cycle of substance misuse. Includes life skills, trauma therapy, art therapy, meditation, yoga, exercise, 12-step work, and counselling.
Notes: File notes to contact directly regarding programming for women with school-aged children.

Description: Highly structured, therapeutic, intensive residential treatment program for women. Evidence-based, trauma-informed, and gender-responsive, with individual and family therapy.
Notes: Low-barrier service; duration 3-6 months.

Description: 90-day residential treatment program for women healing from addiction. Social model, 12-step based, with therapy groups, AA/NA meetings, family support groups, and relapse prevention for alumni.
Notes: Non-smoking; replacement therapy permitted.

Description: Indigenous-focused recovery program ranging from 90 days to 1 year. Includes whole-person programming including life skills, recreation, employment readiness, drum circles, sweats, and healing walks.
Structured, women-centred social learning model. North Burnaby 90-day program offering daily process groups, guided 12-step work, music therapy, yoga, and community meetings.
Community care licensed facility for adult women needing low to moderate 3-5 month residential treatment for problematic substance use. Supports reintegration into community.
Hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
Notes: File notes to contact directly regarding programming for women with school-aged children.

Description: 6-week program; OAT yes; pregnant accepted (2nd trimester)
Notes: 6-week program; OAT yes; pregnant accepted (2nd trimester)

Description: Private residential treatment centre with psychoeducation, process group therapy, individual counselling, wellness programming, medical services, case management, family days, alumni support, and peer recovery supports.

Description: Structured women’s residential recovery home with a 30-day stabilization phase, medication management, required self-help meetings, and OAT/ORT permitted.

Description: Faith-based residential recovery program for men with transitional recovery properties between Chilliwack and Abbotsford.

Description: Clinic providing comprehensive assessment, support and opioid agonist treatment.

Service Type: Rapid Access to Addiction Care Clinic
Age / Eligibility: Self-referral accepted; Care Card not required
Description: Low-barrier outpatient clinic providing temporary stabilization for people seeking treatment for substance use disorders, with transfer to community care for ongoing monitoring and support.
Address: Burrard Building, St. Paul's Hospital, Room 2C-210, 1081 Burrard Street
Notes: St. Paul's RAAC. Walk-in clients may be redirected if clinic is at capacity.

Description: Low-barrier addiction medicine clinic offering same-day assessment and treatment for substance use disorders, including opioids, alcohol, nicotine, GHB, cannabis, stimulants and benzodiazepines.
Notes: DTES same-day addiction treatment hub.

Resource Name: Lighthouse Virtual Substance Use Care Clinic
Description: Free virtual clinic providing telephone-based medical treatment and short-term stabilization for people who use substances, with addiction medicine, nursing, and social work support.
Access Type: Self-referral / provider referral / emergency services referral
Notes: Telephone-based same-day treatment and linkage to community care.

Description: Same-day clinical assessment, personalized substance use care planning, and detox referral line for the VCH region.
Notes: Also coordinates some supported recovery housing referrals.

Description: Free inpatient medical withdrawal management for stable adults, including assessment, counselling, psychoeducational groups, alternate therapies, and medical management for supervised prescription withdrawal.
Notes: Accepts clients on methadone maintenance who are withdrawing from other drugs.

Resource Name: Road to Recovery - St. Paul's Hospital
Organization: Providence Health Care / BC Centre on Substance Use
Age / Eligibility: Greater Vancouver adults seeking substance use care
Description: Integrated continuum of care at St. Paul's Hospital linking withdrawal management, transition beds, treatment, and aftercare through a single coordinated model.
Notes: Includes withdrawal management beds and transition supports.

Resource Name: Emergency Department HUB - St. Paul's Hospital
Organization: St. Paul's Hospital / Providence Health Care
Service Type: Emergency Substance Use / Mental Health Stabilization
Age / Eligibility: Patients presenting to St. Paul's ED with mental health and/or substance use issues
Description: Two-site program providing rapid assessment, treatment, stabilization, and short-stay transitional aftercare for people presenting to the ED with mental health and/or substance use issues.
Access Type: ED presentation / Providence clinician referral for transitional site
Notes: Provides access to the Rapid Access Addiction Clinic (RAAC) and transitional aftercare.

Resource Name: Addiction Services - Downtown Community Health Centre
Description: Assessment, treatment, counselling, education, aftercare, treatment planning, and referral to community resources. Supports clients on methadone maintenance and people concerned about someone else's substance use.
Address: Downtown Community Health Centre, 569 Powell Street
Notes: Interpretation may be available depending on staff availability.

Resource Name: Addiction Medicine - Raven Song Community Health Centre
Age / Eligibility: Adults with opioid addiction living in Vancouver
Description: Provides access to methadone treatment programs with clinical assessment, counselling, specialized treatment planning, aftercare, and follow-up.
Notes: Methadone-focused addiction medicine service.

Resource Name: Substance Use Services - Three Bridges Community Health Centre
Description: Groups, counselling, care coordination, intake consultation, and connections to Daytox/residential treatment and addiction medicine for OAT and safe supply.
Notes: West End / Vancouver adults.

Resource Name: Substance Use Services - Raven Song Community Health Centre
Description: Individual, couple, family, and group addiction counselling with assessment and care planning.
Notes: Counselling-focused outpatient service.

Description: Individual and group addiction counselling using a client-centred, harm-reduction approach, mainly in the DTES and Strathcona communities. Also offers prevention and early intervention programming.
Notes: Service may be available in Cantonese, Hindi, Punjabi, Spanish, and Vietnamese.

Organization: Native Courtworker and Counselling Association of BC (NCCABC)
Description: Culturally appropriate outpatient mental wellness and substance use program offering individual, couple, family, and group counselling, plus wellness and cultural programming.
Address: Lower Mainland Regional Office, 520 Richards Street
Notes: Low-barrier and LGBTQ-friendly.

Service Type: Youth Counselling and Integrated Wellness Centre
Age / Eligibility: Youth and families; psychiatry service for ages 18-24
Description: Free and confidential youth wellness centre offering drop-in counselling and health services; youth peer support workers include people with lived experience of substance use or other life challenges.
Notes: Integrated youth wellness service with drop-in counselling.

Age / Eligibility: People seeking opioid use disorder treatment
Description: Private treatment clinic for opioid use disorder offering methadone, Suboxone, Sublocade, same-day treatment starts, counselling, physician services, referrals, pharmacy services, and free naloxone kits.
Notes: Fee-based service.

Description: Specialized clinic and leader in substance use treatment; first clinic in North America to offer diacetylmorphine (prescription heroin) and hydromorphone within a comprehensive treatment program.
Notes: Highly specialized OUD treatment program.

Population: Indigenous people; others in DTES welcome
Description: Offers health services including medical, counselling, and social services to Aboriginal people in Vancouver; people of all cultures in the DTES are welcome.
Notes: DTES Indigenous health, counselling, and social services.

Service Type: Women's Sober Living / Assisted Living Residency
Age / Eligibility: Women who have completed a minimum 90-day primary care program
Description: 14-bed sober living assisted living residency for women seeking a safe, supportive, structured place to continue recovery.
Notes: Women-only sober living residence. Collaboration between Together We Can and Charlford House.

Age / Eligibility: Completed treatment program and 90 days sober
Description: 20-bed second-stage sobriety support housing with shared space.
Notes: From colleague second-stage list; verify current application details directly.

Description: Recovery-based post-treatment transitional housing with furnished studio apartments and longer-term support.
Notes: From colleague list: up to 2 years; 98 furnished studio apartments; verify directly.

Description: 40 studio units for people who have completed treatment, with private washroom and kitchen.
Access Type: Apply through supportive housing / BC Housing
Notes: From colleague list; verify directly.

Description: Abstinent-based supportive housing with staff support.
Notes: From colleague list: 37 units with staff; verify directly.

Age / Eligibility: 90 days abstinent and/or completion of treatment program; referral required
Description: Second-stage housing for men with required participation in programming and zero tolerance for substances.
Notes: From colleague list; stay may be up to 1 year; verify directly.

Description: Single-room second-stage housing with shared kitchen and bathroom and ongoing life-groups / AA participation.
Notes: From colleague list: 9-12 months; verify directly.

Age / Eligibility: Women seeking support to reduce and/or stop substance use
Description: 38 units of transitional housing for women. Includes spaces for women waiting for treatment after detox and women leaving treatment while seeking long-term housing.
Notes: From colleague list; 10 beds post-detox waiting for treatment and 28 post-treatment housing beds.

Age / Eligibility: Women seeking support to reduce or eliminate substance use
Description: 10-unit specialized transitional housing program for women and their children.
Notes: From colleague list; verify directly.

Age / Eligibility: Women seeking support to reduce or eliminate substance use
Description: 10-unit specialized transitional housing program for women and their children.
Notes: From colleague list; verify directly.

Age / Eligibility: Women seeking support to reduce or eliminate substance use

Population: First Nations, Métis, and Inuit women; women with children under 2 also welcome
Description: 46 units of supportive housing for Indigenous women, including perinatal support connections through Fir Square.
Notes: From colleague list; women with children under age 2 are welcome.

Population: Adults experiencing homelessness and substance use / mental health challenges
Description: Housing First ACT team supporting people facing homelessness, poverty, trauma, mental health challenges, and substance use challenges, with access to housing and multidisciplinary supports.
Notes: Multidisciplinary ACT team with harm reduction and housing focus.
